user_story: As the platform, I want to send a Day-0 welcome email to each newly provisioned subscriber so that they know their assistant is ready and how to connect a messaging channel.
expected_behaviour: Called from apps/orchestrator/services.py at provisioning step 4c after the optional Telegram welcome message is sent. Reads Tenant.welcome_email_sent_at as an idempotency guard — if already non-null, returns False immediately without sending. Skips silently if User.email is blank. Checks LineQuotaState.get().is_exhausted to decide channel_label: 'Telegram or LINE' when quota is available, 'Telegram' otherwise. Renders email/welcome_subject.txt (two subject variants based on telegram_connected), email/welcome_body.txt, and email/welcome_body.html with context: display_name, telegram_connected, settings_url ({FRONTEND_URL}/settings/integrations), video_url (from WELCOME_VIDEO_URL setting, optional), line_quota_available, channel_label. Sends via Django send_mail (DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL). On success stamps Tenant.welcome_email_sent_at via update_fields=['welcome_email_sent_at','updated_at']. Exceptions are caught and logged; callers must not roll back provisioning on failure. Returns True if sent, False on any skip or failure.
dependencies: Mailgun (via Django email backend, DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL), apps/router/models.py:LineQuotaState, Supabase/Postgres (Tenant row update)
inventory_notes: Subject line branches on User.telegram_chat_id: 'Welcome — your assistant just said hi' vs 'Welcome — pick a channel to start chatting'. LINE P.S. postscript only rendered when Telegram is connected AND LINE quota is not exhausted. Optional video_url block gated on WELCOME_VIDEO_URL setting. Fails-closed on LINE quota lookup (omits P.S. rather than risk 429). Provisioning retries are safe because of the idempotency stamp.

user_story: As the platform, I want to record the exact timestamp of a subscriber's first-ever inbound message so that I can measure onboarding activation rates.
expected_behaviour: Called inside apps/router/pending_queue.py:enqueue_message_for_tenant after every PendingMessage creation. Uses a conditional UPDATE: Tenant.objects.filter(id=tenant.id, first_message_at__isnull=True).update(first_message_at=timezone.now()). This means only the first call on a never-messaged tenant writes the stamp; all subsequent calls are no-ops at the DB level. Works across all channels (telegram, line, app) because it's at the PendingMessage chokepoint. No API surface; purely a side effect of enqueueing a message.
inventory_notes: Race-safe via the conditional UPDATE — two concurrent first messages don't overwrite each other. The stamp is never bumped again after the first write. Used to measure onboarding drop-off cohort.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container, I want to mark a feature welcome as delivered after successfully sending the in-chat welcome message so that the deploy-time backfill can skip already-served tenants.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/welcomes/<feature>/. Requires X-NBHD-Internal-Key and X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers matching the per-tenant internal_api_key and URL tenant_id; returns 401 on auth failure. Valid feature values: 'fuel', 'finance', 'core' (case-insensitive strip); returns 400 with error='unknown_feature' and 'valid' list for anything else. Returns 404 if tenant not found. On success sets Tenant.welcomes_sent[feature] = ISO-8601 timestamp now via update_fields=['welcomes_sent','updated_at']. Idempotent — re-calls overwrite the timestamp without error. Returns 200 JSON {feature, sent_at}. Triggering this save also fires the agenda_signals post_save handler which mirrors the welcomes_sent transition into AgendaEngagement.FEATURE_INTRO as COMPLETED (on_commit).
dependencies: apps/integrations/internal_auth.py:validate_internal_runtime_request, Supabase/Postgres (Tenant row update), apps/tenants/agenda_signals.py (post_save mirror to AgendaEngagement)
inventory_notes: Uses AllowAny DRF permission class; authorization is entirely in the internal-key validation. AgendaEngagement mirror fires via transaction.on_commit so it doesn't fire inside test TestCase transactions — only in TransactionTestCase.

user_story: As the platform, I want to automatically record an AgendaEngagement COMPLETED event when a per-feature welcome is delivered so that the engagement renderer can suppress repeat onboarding prompts consistently.
expected_behaviour: Wired via pre_save + @receiver(post_save) on Tenant in apps/tenants/agenda_signals.py. pre_save captures the prior welcomes_sent dict from DB into _PRE_SAVE_WELCOMES keyed by tenant PK. post_save diffs current vs prior: for each key that transitioned from absent/null to a timestamp, schedules mark_surfaced (kind=FEATURE_INTRO, item_id=feature, signal='welcome_delivered') + mark_state (state=COMPLETED) via transaction.on_commit. Idempotent: only transitions (null → timestamp) trigger the mirror; stable non-null values on subsequent saves are no-ops. Does not fire inside TestCase (on_commit deferred).
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (AgendaEngagement writes via apps/tenants/agenda_service.py), Django signal framework
inventory_notes: Uses _PRE_SAVE_WELCOMES module-level dict — not safe for multiprocessing without reset; transient signals under WSGI/multi-worker are fine because each request has one pre+post cycle. The mirror is best-effort; exceptions are logged, not re-raised.

feature: Promo campaign creation and email fan-out (send_promo_campaign management command)
user_story: As an operator, I want to create a promotional campaign and email per-user HMAC-signed redemption links to eligible subscribers so that I can offer trial extensions for retention campaigns.
expected_behaviour: management command: python manage.py send_promo_campaign --code <slug> --kind trial_extension --days <int> --valid-until <ISO8601> [--dry-run]. Audience filter: Users with a tenant AND (status=ACTIVE + is_trial=True OR status=SUSPENDED + stripe_subscription_id=''), excluding blank email and PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL. --dry-run prints audience without creating the row or sending. Creates PromoCampaign via get_or_create(code=code) — first run wins the audience snapshot (audience_snapshot JSON with user_ids + captured_at_count), subsequent re-runs reuse the same row and email everyone again (idempotent send; double-redemption is blocked at the view layer). For each user, calls make_promo_token(campaign_code, user_id) then builds {FRONTEND_URL}/promo/redeem?code=<code>&token=<signed> and sends using email/privacy_rotation_2026/email_2_{subject,body}.txt/.html templates with context {display_name, promo_url, valid_until}. Per-user email failures are caught, logged, and counted; the command continues to remaining users.
dependencies: Mailgun (via Django email backend), Supabase/Postgres (PromoCampaign create/get, User/Tenant queries), apps/tenants/promo_signing.py:make_promo_token, QStash (dispatch via cron/views TASK_MAP)
inventory_notes: send_promo_campaign_task() is a QStash-dispatched wrapper hardcoding code='privacy-zdr-2026', days=14, valid_until='2026-06-24T00:00:00+00:00' — designed to fire once. The command itself is reusable for future campaigns with different parameters. Re-running the command with the same --code does NOT widen the audience snapshot but DOES re-email the original audience (redemption idempotency is enforced by the view, not the command).

feature: Promo HMAC token signing and verification
user_story: As the platform, I want to sign per-user redemption tokens for promo campaigns so that only the intended recipient can redeem the trial extension from their email inbox without needing to be logged in.
expected_behaviour: apps/tenants/promo_signing.py exports make_promo_token(campaign_code, user_id) and verify_promo_token(expected_campaign_code, token). make_promo_token uses Django Signer(salt='nbhd.promos.v1').sign('{code}:{user_id}') — deterministic for the same inputs so re-sends produce identical tokens. verify_promo_token unsigns the token, splits on ':', and checks that the embedded campaign_code matches expected_campaign_code; returns user_id string on success, None on BadSignature, mismatched code, or malformed payload. Expiry is NOT enforced in the token — the campaign's valid_until on the PromoCampaign row is the deadline.
inventory_notes: Salt 'nbhd.promos.v1' scopes promo tokens to prevent substitution with other signed payloads in the codebase. Token is stable across re-sends of the same campaign for the same user — useful for partial retry after Mailgun outage. Mismatched campaign code is treated as forgery rather than silent pass.

user_story: As a subscriber who received a promo email, I want to click the redemption link to extend my trial so that I can continue using my assistant without subscribing immediately.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/promos/redeem/?code=<campaign_code>&token=<signed_token> — unauthenticated (AllowAny). Validation chain: (1) missing code or token → 302 to {FRONTEND_URL}/promo/redeemed?status=invalid; (2) campaign not found → invalid; (3) timezone.now() >= campaign.valid_until → 302 status=expired; (4) verify_promo_token fails → invalid; (5) user not found → invalid; (6) user has no tenant → records PromoRedemption(outcome=NO_TENANT) → invalid; (7) tenant has stripe_subscription_id → records PromoRedemption(outcome=ALREADY_SUBSCRIBED) → 302 status=active_subscription. Success path: base = max(now, tenant.trial_ends_at if future else now); new_trial_ends_at = base + timedelta(days=campaign.extension_days). Inserts PromoRedemption row OUTSIDE atomic block first (unique_together(campaign,user) catches concurrent double-clicks → returns 302 status=already without updating tenant). Then wraps tenant update in atomic: sets trial_ends_at, is_trial=True, status=ACTIVE. On tenant update failure: deletes the redemption row to allow retry, returns status=invalid. On success: 302 status=success.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PromoCampaign lookup, PromoRedemption insert, Tenant update), apps/tenants/promo_signing.py:verify_promo_token, Next.js frontend /promo/redeemed route (redirect target)
inventory_notes: Insert-first pattern (redemption row outside atomic, tenant update inside) avoids broken Django savepoint state when catching IntegrityError inside an active atomic block. Paying subscribers (stripe_subscription_id non-empty) are blocked even if they made it past the audience filter in the send command — is_trial is not re-set for them.

user_story: As an operator, I want to send a rendered preview of any campaign email to my own inbox before a fleet-wide send so that I can visually verify the HTML renders correctly across email clients.
expected_behaviour: management command: python manage.py preview_email --kind <1|2> --to <email> [--display-name <name>]. kind=1 renders email/privacy_rotation_2026/email_1_{subject,body}.{txt,html} with sample reset_url 'https://neighborhoodunited.org/reset-password?uid=PREVIEW&token=PREVIEW'; kind=2 renders email/privacy_rotation_2026/email_2_{subject,body}.{txt,html} with sample promo_url and valid_until=2026-06-24. Prepends '[PREVIEW]' to subject. Sends via send_mail to the --to address. Invalid kind raises CommandError (only 1 and 2 accepted). Also available as a QStash task (preview_email_task) callable with kind, to, display_name kwargs — allows operator to trigger a preview remotely without container exec.
dependencies: Mailgun (via Django email backend), QStash (optional remote dispatch)
inventory_notes: preview_email_task validates kind in (1, 2) and raises ValueError for invalid values — QStash will retry on ValueError unless the task is idempotent-flagged. The sample promo_url in kind=2 preview points to the live redeem flow but the token 'PREVIEW' won't pass HMAC verification.

feature: Fleet-wide password rotation with reset email (privacy campaign)
user_story: As an operator running a privacy hygiene campaign, I want to force-rotate every subscriber's password and email them a reset link so that users must set a fresh password and all outstanding JWTs are invalidated.
expected_behaviour: management command: python manage.py rotate_all_passwords --reason=<string> [--dry-run] [--since <ISO8601>]. Excludes PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL user from rotation (warns if not set). For each remaining User: skips if no email, skips if password_last_changed_at >= --since cutoff (idempotency on retry). Calls user.set_unusable_password() (which bumps password_last_changed_at via model override and therefore invalidates all outstanding JWTs for that user via the pw_iat JWT claim), saves with update_fields=['password','password_last_changed_at']. Then calls _send_reset_email: generates Django default_token_generator.make_token + urlsafe_base64_encode(user.pk) and renders email/privacy_rotation_2026/email_1_{subject,body}.{txt,html} with reset_url={FRONTEND_URL}/reset-password?uid=&token=. Per-user email failures are logged but do NOT prevent password rotation (user is locked out until manual resend). --dry-run prints would-rotate list without any mutations. Also registered in QStash TASK_MAP as 'rotate_all_passwords' wrapping rotate_all_passwords_task().
dependencies: Mailgun (via Django email backend), Supabase/Postgres (User.password + password_last_changed_at update), Django default_token_generator, QStash (optional dispatch)
inventory_notes: CRITICAL: if email send fails after the password rotation, the user is locked out with no usable password and no reset link — they need a manual resend. The --since cutoff defaults to now() at command start so a single run rotates everyone; subsequent runs with the same --since value are idempotent. Users with no email address are skipped (rotated users with email only).

feature: Privacy rotation campaign email 1 (password reset template)
user_story: As a subscriber who received the privacy rotation email, I want to click the reset link so that I can set a new password and regain access to the dashboard.
expected_behaviour: Template set: email/privacy_rotation_2026/email_1_{subject,body}.txt + email_1_body.html. Subject: 'Action required: reset your NBHD United password'. Context vars: display_name, reset_url. The reset_url points to {FRONTEND_URL}/reset-password?uid=<b64>&token=<django_token> where the token is from default_token_generator.make_token(user) on the user's newly set unusable password hash. The token auto-invalidates when the user sets a new password (hash changes) or after PASSWORD_RESET_TIMEOUT (Django default 3 days). Rendered only by the rotate_all_passwords command (not the self-service forgot-password flow, which uses email/password_reset_body.html).
inventory_notes: Dark-themed Constellation aesthetic HTML with Outlook VML fallback for the CTA button and gradient headline. Separate from the self-service password reset email (email/password_reset_body.html) which has different template structure and context.

feature: Privacy rotation campaign email 2 (promo trial extension template)
user_story: As a subscriber targeted by a retention campaign, I want to receive a promotional email with a trial extension offer so that I can redeem extra free days.
expected_behaviour: Template set: email/privacy_rotation_2026/email_2_{subject,body}.txt + email_2_body.html. Subject: 'Your data stays yours — and 14 days, on us'. Context vars: display_name, promo_url ({FRONTEND_URL}/promo/redeem?code=&token=), valid_until (rendered as 'Good through ...' deadline). Used by both send_promo_campaign management command and the preview_email kind=2 command. The valid_until is passed from the PromoCampaign.valid_until field so copy stays synchronized with actual deadline.
inventory_notes: The valid_until template variable was introduced to fix a copy-drift bug where the original templates hardcoded 'June 6, 2026' and went stale as the campaign deadline was extended.

user_story: As a subscriber who forgot their password, I want to request a password reset email so that I can regain access to my dashboard.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/request/ with body {email}. AllowAny. Rate-limited: 3 requests per email per hour (key pwd_reset_email:<email>) AND 5 requests per IP per hour (key pwd_reset_ip:<IP>, from X-Forwarded-For or REMOTE_ADDR). Both buckets checked before any DB work; returns HTTP 429 on either limit breach. Timing-safe: always returns 200 {detail: '...'} regardless of whether the email exists, is active, or fails — account enumeration is prevented. If user exists and is_active: generates uid=urlsafe_base64_encode(user.pk) + token=default_token_generator.make_token(user), calls _send_password_reset_email which renders email/password_reset_{subject,body}.txt + password_reset_body.html with context {user, reset_url, display_name} and sends via Django send_mail. Email send failures are caught and logged; the 200 response is still returned. Cache unavailability fails-open on rate limiting (logs warning, proceeds).
dependencies: Mailgun (via Django email backend), Redis/cache (rate-limit counters), Supabase/Postgres (User lookup), Django default_token_generator
inventory_notes: Rate limit window is 1 hour (PASSWORD_RESET_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS=3600). Cache failure fails-open (does not block the request) to avoid locking real users out during a Redis blip. The reset link points to {FRONTEND_URL}/reset-password?uid=&token= handled by PasswordResetConfirmView.

user_story: As a subscriber who clicked a password reset link, I want to set a new password and be automatically signed in so that I land on the dashboard without a second login step.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/confirm/ with body {uid, token, new_password}. AllowAny. Returns 400 if any of uid, token, new_password is missing. Decodes uid via urlsafe_base64_decode; 400 if user not found or uid malformed. Validates token via default_token_generator.check_token(user, token) — tokens auto-invalidate on password change and after PASSWORD_RESET_TIMEOUT (default 3 days); 400 with 'Reset link is invalid or has expired.' if invalid. Validates new_password via Django validate_password(new_password, user=user) — returns 400 with joined validation messages on failure. Calls user.set_password(new_password) which bumps password_last_changed_at (model override). Saves with update_fields=['password','password_last_changed_at'] — MUST include password_last_changed_at to persist the stamp. Mints a fresh JWT pair via EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token(user) (which includes the pw_iat claim) and returns 200 {access, refresh}.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (User password update), Django default_token_generator, apps/tenants/serializers.py:EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer (pw_iat JWT claim)
inventory_notes: Must use EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token (not RefreshToken.for_user) to mint the post-reset JWT, otherwise the pw_iat claim is absent and JWTAuthenticationWithRLS immediately rejects the brand-new token for users with a non-null password_last_changed_at. update_fields MUST include password_last_changed_at — omitting it silently drops the stamp which defeats force-logout.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to receive a clear, branded password reset email so that I can identify the link and click it confidently.
expected_behaviour: Template set: email/password_reset_subject.txt (subject: 'Reset your NBHD United password'), email/password_reset_body.txt, email/password_reset_body.html. Context vars: user (User model instance), reset_url ({FRONTEND_URL}/reset-password?uid=&token=), display_name. Rendered only by the self-service PasswordResetRequestView flow (not the fleet rotate_all_passwords command which uses the privacy_rotation_2026/email_1 templates). Dark-themed Constellation aesthetic HTML with NBHD star icon.
inventory_notes: Separate template set from privacy_rotation_2026/email_1 — different structure and no display_name from campaign context. The html template uses autoescape on and a minimal dark card layout.

user_story: As an operator, I want a database record for each promotional campaign with a unique code, kind, extension_days, valid_until deadline, and audience snapshot so that redemptions can be validated and audited.
expected_behaviour: apps/tenants/promo_models.py:PromoCampaign — table 'promo_campaigns'. Fields: id (UUID PK), code (SlugField max_length=64, unique), kind (TextChoices: TRIAL_EXTENSION), extension_days (PositiveSmallIntegerField), valid_until (DateTimeField), audience_snapshot (JSONField, shape {user_ids: [...], captured_at_count: int}), created_at (auto). Ordered by -created_at. The code field is used in redemption URLs and token signing — must be URL-safe. The audience_snapshot is written once at campaign creation time by the send_promo_campaign command (first-run wins semantics via get_or_create); re-runs don't overwrite it.
inventory_notes: Only one Kind value exists (TRIAL_EXTENSION) — model docstring notes future: STRIPE_COUPON, FEATURE_UNLOCK. The unique code constraint is load-bearing for the get_or_create idempotency in the send command.

user_story: As the platform, I want to record each promo redemption attempt with its outcome and the resulting trial_ends_at so that double-redemptions are blocked at the database layer and the audit trail is queryable.
expected_behaviour: apps/tenants/promo_models.py:PromoRedemption — table 'promo_redemptions'. Fields: id (UUID PK), campaign (FK PromoCampaign), user (FK AUTH_USER_MODEL), redeemed_at (auto), outcome (TextChoices: EXTENDED, ALREADY_SUBSCRIBED, NO_TENANT), new_trial_ends_at (DateTimeField nullable). unique_together=[('campaign','user')] is the load-bearing double-click guard — IntegrityError on the second insert is caught by redeem_promo and returns 'already' status. Index on (user, -redeemed_at). Outcome NO_TENANT and ALREADY_SUBSCRIBED rows are recorded for audit even though the trial is not extended.
inventory_notes: _record_redemption wraps the insert in transaction.atomic() (savepoint) to contain IntegrityError without poisoning a parent transaction — critical in test environments where the outer TestCase transaction is already open.

user_story: As the platform, I want to track per-feature welcome delivery timestamps on each tenant so that deploy-time backfills can skip already-served tenants and retry only those where the welcome failed silently.
expected_behaviour: Tenant.welcomes_sent is a JSONField (default={}) storing per-feature ISO-8601 timestamps, e.g. {'fuel': '2026-05-07T...', 'finance': '...', 'core': '...'}. Set by RuntimeWelcomeMarkView (POST from container after successful welcome send) and also read/written by apps/fuel/views.py and apps/core/views.py when scheduling the welcome cron (they may pre-populate the key if the cron is already registered). The deploy-time backfill skips tenants whose welcomes_sent[feature] is already set. Cleared implicitly only by explicit removal — not cleared on monthly resets. Changes to this field trigger the agenda_signals post_save handler which mirrors transitions into AgendaEngagement.
entry_points: apps/tenants/models.py:Tenant.welcomes_sent ; apps/tenants/runtime_views.py:RuntimeWelcomeMarkView ; apps/fuel/views.py (fuel welcome cron scheduling) ; apps/core/views.py (core welcome cron scheduling)
inventory_notes: Valid feature keys are 'fuel', 'finance', 'core' (enforced by RuntimeWelcomeMarkView). The JSON field persists across months and container restarts. The backfill logic lives in the respective feature's task/view, not in tenants.

feature: Tenant quota warning and exhaustion email stamps
user_story: As the platform, I want to track when quota warning and exhaustion emails were last sent to each tenant so that I can prevent duplicate notifications when usage hovers near the threshold.
expected_behaviour: Tenant model carries two DateTimeField idempotency markers: cost_warn_sent_at (90%-of-cap warning email) and cost_exhausted_email_sent_at (cap-exhausted email). Both are null until the corresponding email fires; both are cleared to None by reset_monthly_counters (apps/tenants/services.py) at the start of each billing month. The actual email-sending logic lives in apps/router/billing_quota_handlers.py:fire_threshold_emails_if_crossed and apps/billing/management/commands/reconcile_openrouter_spend.py, not in the tenants app itself — these fields are the storage layer only.
inventory_notes: These are storage/idempotency fields only — the actual email template rendering and sending happens in apps/router and apps/billing. The monthly counter reset (clear-to-null) ensures the first over-threshold usage each billing month always triggers a fresh notification.
